title: Whats the exact height of an EVA
display_title: Whats the exact height of an EVA
wikitext:
[]: What's the exact height of an EVA?             Quote Post by ed » Sat Oct 24, 2009 7:16 am it puzzles me and many fans..

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sat Oct 24, 2009 7:25 am It varies. Between 40 and 200 meters is the usual statement. http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=4265 http://www.evacommentary.org/appendix/angel-size-comparison.jpg These provide more info.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Oct 24, 2009 8:44 am

[]:             Quote Post by ed » Sat Oct 24, 2009 8:51 am thanks you and i decide to watch the REBUILD1.10 again,maybe there are some clues.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 24, 2009 9:00 am That chart is based on the SRW game height only of 40m, which isn't really reliable at all since another SRW game assigns it a height of only 15m, both of which are ridiculous. (see the already linked thread for more details). There has never been an official height from Gainax about the TV/EoE. It's all SRW values and people use that as "official". The 40m value only barely fits in the bottom range of the variable height for only like half a dozen cuts. The only official height to ever come out of Gainax is for the Rebuild Evas which are a more appropriate 80m to the tip of the pylons.

[]:             Quote Post by ed » Sat Oct 24, 2009 9:09 am GJ As we kown ANNO is an automan fan,i used to think EVA may more like automan,so it is 40 to 60m.But when EVAoo with the spear(100m+),it seems..it is 80m or more..so it puzzles me..

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sat Oct 24, 2009 9:20 am There isn't a single hight of the evas, as was mentioned before it varies greatly: in episode 1 (for example) unit 01 was as tall as the tallest buildings in Tokyo 3. In episode 16, the evas were so much shorter than the buildings that unit 02 could climb one of them and it was significantly higher. It really depends what scene you're looking at

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 24, 2009 10:05 am toe mash wrote: It really depends what scene you're looking at

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Mon Apr 11, 2011 1:38 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: That chart is based on the SRW game height only of 40m, which isn't really reliable at all since another SRW game assigns it a height of only 15m, both of which are ridiculous. (see the already linked thread for more details). There has never been an official height from Gainax about the TV/EoE. It's all SRW values and people use that as "official". The 40m value only barely fits in the bottom range of the variable height for only like half a dozen cuts. The only official height to ever come out of Gainax is for the Rebuild Evas which are a more appropriate 80m to the tip of the pylons.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 3:34 am toe mash wrote: in episode 1 (for example) unit 01 was as tall as the tallest buildings in Tokyo 3. In episode 16, the evas were so much shorter than the buildings that unit 02 could climb one of them and it was significantly higher.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Mon Apr 11, 2011 6:46 am The fluctuating size range is well demonstrated in this image . However, it's worthwhile to remember that mecha sizes tend to be all over the place even in shows that are supposed to feature fixed canon dimensions. Char's Counterattack had a movie budget, and its attention to detail is staggering, but there's still a lot of baffling shit going on in it .

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 12:39 pm I always went with a rounded 40 meter figure (I can't remember where I first read the number, but it seemed consistent with the cage and Kaworu scenes). I got into a lot of fights with Gundam fans in my younger days and was amused at the idea of an Eva kicking one like a football due to the difference in scale Now I am a little amused that the official figure has doubled and the theoretical figure quintupled. Of course, as the debates went on, someone always had to bring up some Gundam that could destroy planets or blow up a sun, so it was never conclusive.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 1:58 pm LordEvan88 wrote: I always went with a rounded 40 meter figure (I can't remember where I first read the number, but it seemed consistent with the cage and Kaworu scenes).

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Mon Apr 11, 2011 7:53 pm Maybe in reality EVAs DO fluctuate in size (pilot stays the same) depending on the situation. Eg. Need to fight Sachiel- size adjusts to moderate(EVA 01); need to break warship in half- size needs to be bigger(EVA 02)!! Maybe that's why the creators don't mention the sizes.....

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Apr 11, 2011 7:55 pm The size of Eva in NGE fluctuates in direct proportion to how much the size of Asuka's boobs fluctuates.

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Mon Apr 11, 2011 7:57 pm Grimmjow wrote: The size of Eva in NGE fluctuates in direct proportion to how much the size of Asuka's boobs fluctuates.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:58 pm More accurately, the Eva's size fluctuates in order to provide a cool visual with the scenery around it. It's artistic license and an acceptable break from reality. Although, if I had to give a measurement I'd say 40m.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Apr 12, 2011 2:56 am There are some shots in EoE where Eva-01 must be kilometers tall in terms of height just sayin'

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Apr 12, 2011 3:07 am Xard wrote: There are some shots in EoE where Eva-01 must be kilometers tall in terms of height just sayin'

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 12, 2011 4:12 am Monk Ed wrote: Really? Where?
